substep.title: Dispatch Children in Parallel
substep.action:
Dispatch every composed child prompt via the Task tool in a SINGLE message — that is what makes them parallel. Do not wait between dispatches; dispatch all, then wait for all to return. Total wall-clock = max(individual times), not sum.
A single-question lookup dispatches one child; that is fine. Parallelism applies when more than one child is dispatched.

substep.title: Validate Returns and Sharpen-Redispatch
substep.action:
Inspect each child's return. A crisp return cites concrete evidence (file:line, URL, thoughts/ alias). A vague return ("found some files", "looks like there might be") is unusable — redispatch that single child with a sharper prompt (tighter scope, more specific question, explicit output shape) before synthesis. Do not paper over a vague return in the synthesis.
A return of "no matches found" is NOT vague — it is a negative finding. Keep it; it becomes a cited answer in 3c.

substep.title: Synthesize per Question
substep.action:
For each question, synthesize the corresponding child's findings into an answer:
- State the fact, not a transcript. Do NOT paste raw child output. - Cite inline: file:line, URL, or thoughts/ alias for every claim. - Record silence as data: a negative finding ships as a cited answer ("searched {scope}; no definition of X found — codebase-locator returned zero matches"). - Attach a confidence level (high / medium / low) reflecting evidence quality. - Note any gap: what the question asked that the evidence could not answer.
When two children return contradictory facts for related questions, surface the contradiction explicitly rather than picking a side.
